documentPurpose: LLM-readable project context for the ACS remake. This file summarizes the current architecture, data model, flows, milestone status, quality expectations, and future AI extension points so an AI assistant can reason about the project without relying only on prose PDFs.

project.name: ACS
project.fullName: Adventure Construction Set inspired construction kit
project.goal: Build a modern, extensible adventure construction environment inspired by 1980s ACS while keeping game rules separate from presentation so later HD 2D, 3D, richer audio, realtime modes, and AI-assisted authoring remain possible.
corePrinciples: Runtime rules and presentation assets are separate.
Game content is structured data, not hardcoded behavior.
Reusable game concepts should become objects with stable ids and editor CRUD rather than loose strings.
Editor UI should follow a hierarchical design flow and show only the relevant panel for the current task.
Documentation, screenshots, PDFs, and AI-readable context should be refreshed as milestones add features.
New or worsened functions over cyclomatic complexity 8 should trigger refactor work. SOLID regressions should be avoided.
Codex collaboration history is tracked in docs/codex-chat-log.json so future LLMs can recover user decisions and current obligations.

primaryApps.role: Browser play mode and browser editor UI.
Small API/data persistence surface for project storage.

primaryPackages.role: Shared TypeScript domain model for AdventurePackage, maps, entities, quests, assets, visual manifests, presentation settings, and starter libraries.
Raw package normalization, defaults, compatibility migration, and content shape cleanup.
Renderer-agnostic game engine for commands, movement, interactions, triggers, enemy turns, state, and events.
Canvas/runtime presentation layer, including classic ACS-inspired visual mode.
Pure editor operations for changing adventure data before browser UI wiring.
Milestone 26 boundary for built-in starter library source metadata, starter snapshots, and custom library export helpers.
Save/load format wrappers around RuntimeSnapshot and local persistence helpers.

playMode: Classic ACS visual mode and debug grid mode render the same runtime state.
Keyboard-first movement and interaction dispatch commands into runtime-core.
Turn pacing prevents enemies from moving on every single player keypress.
Dialogue can be displayed in the classic message band while debug mode can show richer debug dialogue UI.
editorMode: Focused editor areas: Adventure, World Atlas, Map Workspace, Libraries, Logic, Test and Publish.
Map workspace supports brush-style tile painting, entity placement/movement, exits, and trigger markers.
Libraries hold entities, items, skills, flags, quests, traits, spells, tiles, dialogue, custom objects, and assets as organized object classes.
Libraries are moving toward a parent-child drilldown: library type, category/class, object, then full-width details below.
milestone24Assets: Adventure presentation settings include splash asset id, starting music cue id, and intro text.
Classic visual manifests can contain editable 8x8 pixel sprite definitions with palette, pixels, usage, tags, and genre tags.
The editor exposes an Assets library panel with splash/music selectors, intro text, pixel sprite selector, palette selector, grid painting, and preview canvases.

The sample adventure includes seven starter genre packs: fantasy shrine, science-fiction data core, modern spy, superhero rooftops, science-fantasy gate, supernatural case file, and urban fantasy alley.

Starter libraries were expanded with ACS-inspired generic things, creatures, weapons, armor, keys, treasure, support NPCs, thief/slinker/fighter archetypes, shops, traps, stairs, signs, chests, bridges, and teleport pads.
milestone25Diagnostics: editor-core exposes createAuthoringDiagnostics for reusable authoring diagnostics
Test & Publish renders Authoring Diagnostics and Playtest Scenarios cards
tools/playtest-smoke.mjs validates and simulates start state, Oracle interaction, shrine reward, item grant, tile change, quest stage, and exit travel
milestone26LibraryPortability: packages/default-content exports builtInStarterLibrarySource metadata for application-owned starter libraries.
packages/default-content exports createLibraryCollections, createStarterLibrarySnapshot, createCustomLibraryExport, listStarterPacksByGenre, and listCustomLibraryObjectCounts.
Built-in starter libraries, adventure-local objects, and reusable custom library exports are documented as separate storage layers.
futurePublishingModes: Publishing should transform immutable releases into artifacts without mutating the original project draft or release snapshot.
forkableProject exports should include editable adventure source, authoring metadata, custom libraries, provenance, visual manifests, assets, and remix/fork licensing metadata.
standalonePlayable exports should include runtime-only package data, required assets, a play shell, distribution metadata, and no editor UI, draft history, source notes, or unused starter content.
A future packages/publishing boundary should own createForkableProjectExport, createStandaloneRuntimeExport, collectRuntimeAssets, pruneUnusedAuthoringData, and validatePublishArtifact.
Standalone export should start as a static web bundle and can later be wrapped for desktop without changing runtime-core.
currentPlanningCorrections: Exit is the underlying map-link record used by runtime travel. Portal is a designer-facing presentation/genre label for a tile, object, trigger, or exit that behaves like a magical/science-fiction passage. They can share traversal machinery while staying distinct in editor language.

Stacked tile effects are not yet complete as a rich first-class authoring model. The roadmap now calls for ordered action stacks on one cell: media/splash, message, give item, take item, stat boost, spawn/despawn NPC, tile change, quest update, sound cue, and portal travel.

All reusable gameplay values should become CRUD-enabled objects with categories, stable ids, duplicate warnings, and validation. Free-text lists are only temporary notes, not reusable game references.
Play mode should keep immediate state visible and add an open-at-need player profile panel for inventory, skills, traits, abilities, active effects, and quest-linked objects.

Default starter libraries should move out of sampleAdventure into reusable library data. Adventures may include custom additions or overrides, and designers should be able to import/export reusable custom library packs.

Editor workspaces should progressively disclose task-specific panels. Map Workspace should show only tile controls while painting tiles, only entity controls while placing or moving entities, and only exit/portal controls while editing travel links.

runtimeFlows.name: Move command
steps: Browser key handler maps an arrow/WASD key to a runtime command.
Game engine dispatches command to GameSession.
Movement helpers validate target map cell, blocked tiles, entities, exits, and triggers.
State is updated if movement is valid.
Enemy turn system advances according to authored turn pacing and behavior profiles.
Runtime events are emitted.
Browser renders the new state through the selected visual mode.

runtimeFlows.name: Inspect or interact command
steps: Browser key handler sends inspect/interact command to runtime-core.
Runtime resolves adjacent or current interactable target.
Dialogue, trigger, item gate, quest stage, teleport, reward, or tile-change effects are executed as structured actions.
State and event log update.
Classic mode writes messages to the bottom band; debug mode may expose richer debug UI.

runtimeFlows.name: Pixel sprite edit
steps: Designer opens Libraries > Assets.
Editor UI selects a ClassicPixelSpriteDefinition from the active classic visual manifest.
Designer selects a palette index and paints a grid cell.
editor-core setClassicPixelSpritePixel updates the sprite pixel array by stable sprite id.
Editor rerenders the grid, small preview, and large preview immediately.

editorFlows.name: Libraries drilldown
steps: Choose Library Type such as Entities, Items, Tiles, Quests, Dialogue, or Assets.
Choose a category/class for that type.
Choose a concrete object.
Edit details in the full-width details panel below the drilldown columns.
Objects with graphics should eventually expose graphics assignment and editing directly from this details surface.

editorFlows.name: Map workspace
steps: Select a map without leaving the workspace.
Choose a tile brush and paint multiple cells.
Place or move entity instances that reference entity definitions.
Attach exits and trigger markers to selected cells.
Playtest the selected map and validate references.

milestone24Audit.item: Adventure presentation settings for splash screen, music cue, and intro text
Manifest-backed classic pixel sprites with palette and pixel grid
Assets editor panel with splash/music selectors and compact 8x8 pixel painting grid
Stocked starter genre packs for fantasy, sci-fi, modern spy, superhero, science fantasy, supernatural, and urban fantasy
Genre-specific groups for reusable tiles, entities, items, skills, traits, spells, and assets
Full object-specific graphics editing for every visual object type
milestone24Audit.status: planned, not part of original Milestone 24 completion

milestone24Audit.item: Library UI parent-child drilldown with full-width detail editor below
milestone24Audit.status: partially scaffolded after Milestone 24; planned for Milestone 27 completion

requirements: Provider interface separate from domain/runtime/editor core.
Adapters for any LLM or agent API that can return structured proposals.
Schema validation, reference checks, duplicate warnings, provenance, and human review before mutation.
Use normal AdventurePackage objects as output.
Support full adventure drafting, partial scene expansion, and gap filling.
Respect designer source notes, genre constraints, starter packs, and validation.

aiNpcBrain.status: planned for optional AI runtime phase
aiNpcBrain.description: Designer-authored context that guides an optional AI provider when speaking or proposing behavior for an NPC.
guardrails: AI text may enrich dialogue, but game-state changes must be validated actions.
Deterministic behavior remains default and offline-safe.
Cost, cooldown, safety, and testability controls are mandatory.

aiNpcEnvironmentInteraction.status: planned for optional AI runtime phase, with actor-action readiness work before AI execution
currentReadiness: Player actions are structured commands, but command handling is currently player-centered.
EnemyTurnSystem moves enemies but does not use player-equivalent item, trigger, portal, or support actions.
TriggerSystem does not yet receive actor context, so permissions and effects cannot distinguish player versus NPC actor.
requiredCapabilities: ActorCommand or RuntimeActionProposal model shared by player, deterministic NPCs, and AI NPCs.
Actor identity in runtime validation so commands apply to the correct entity position, inventory, faction, and map.
Designer-authored capability profiles for support, informational, random, rival, and antagonist NPCs.
Explicit allow/deny permissions for items, triggers, portals/exits, maps, object classes, and action categories.
Actor-aware trigger context and actor inventories/equipment.
Validated action proposals only; AI must not directly mutate state.

sharedActorActions.principle: Players and AI/NPC actors should use the same validated runtime action functions; individual items, exits, triggers, maps, and action categories can still be player-only, NPC-only, blocked, all-actor, or explicit allow-list.

afterEachMilestone: Update User Guide tutorial with creative feature exercises and current screenshots.
Update System Reference with architecture, sequence diagrams, implementation details, and screenshots.
Regenerate PDFs when human-facing guide/reference documents change.
Update this LLM-readable project context with new architecture, data, flows, status, and known gaps.

contextualVisualEditors.principle: Any object with a visual binding should expose graphical editing in that object detail panel, not only in a generic Assets panel.

milestone25Audit.item: Reusable authoring diagnostics model and builder
Visible diagnostics and scenario prompts in Test & Publish

milestone25Audit.status: complete for Markdown/HTML; PDF regeneration depends on an available local renderer
